title: endospermum moluccanum
intro: Endospermum moluccanum, also known as the Polynesian spearwood or Malayan staff tree, is a tropical tree native to Southeast Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to the presence of a compound called lupeol, which has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Endospermum moluccanum include reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and soothing skin irritations. The plant is often prepared as a decoction, tincture, or powder for herbal remedies, and is also used in traditional medicine in various parts of the world.
